content.problemStatement:
There is a string SSS of size NNN consisting of numbers between 000 and 999, and some blanks represented by ???.

Let MMM be the number of blanks. Then, the following process repeats MMM times :


A uniformly random number XXX between 000 and 999 is generated.
You choose iii such that SiS_iSi​ is blank and change SiS_iSi​ to XXX.
You want to maximize the final value of the string SSS at the end of the process. Here, the value of a string SSS is the number formed by considering the string SSS as a decimal number. (Note that there are no blanks left in SSS)

Find the expected value of SiS_iSi​ for all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N at the end of the process if you try to maximize the value of SSS.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of input contains NNN - the length of the string SSS
The second line of input contains SSS - the string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN real numbers : E1,E2,E3,...,ENE_1, E_2, E_3, ..., E_NE1​,E2​,E3​,...,EN​ where EiE_iEi​ represents the expected value of SiS_iSi​.


Your answer will be accepted if the absolute or relative error in each of the answers is at most 10−610^{-6}10−6.
constraints: There exists at least one iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N) such that Si=?S_i = ?Si​=?
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1032 \cdot 10^32⋅103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There is only one blank. So whatever appears in the first chance, we must place it in the only blank presents in SSS. Since 000 to 999 is equiprobable, the expected value is (0+1+2+...+9)10=4.5\frac{(0 + 1 + 2 + ... + 9)}{10} = 4.510(0+1+2+...+9)​=4.5. The expected value at the 111st position is just 111 because that is fixed.
Test Case 2 : Here, depending on what the first generated number is, we will either place it in the 111st or 222nd blank. For example, if the first generated number is 999, we will obviously place it in the first blank as our goal is to maximize the value of SSS.
